title: Pentaphylloides dryadanthoides
intro: Pentaphylloides dryadanthoides, also known as dryad's ear, is a flowering plant species in the family Rosaceae native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents of Pentaphylloides dryadanthoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or used in infusions, and it is also sometimes incorporated into modern herbal remedies, such as tinctures and capsules.
